---
title: "Weekly Progress"
subtitle: "{{WEEK_START}} – {{WEEK_END}}"
date: "{{REPORT_DATE}}"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dark
    slide-number: true
    incremental: false
    toc: false
    progress: true
    chalkboard: false
    html-math-method: katex
    width: 1280
    height: 800
---

<!--
USAGE
- Replace every {{TOKEN}} placeholder; delete this comment when done.
- Slide bodies: 3–6 short bullets, ≤ 15 words each.
- Speaker notes (::: {.notes}) follow this cadence:
    **Bold subheader** mirrors a slide bullet group.
    1–3 plain bullets expand each slide bullet.
    Final block is always **Implication** — the one sentence to leave with.
- Mirror this 3-slide pattern per finding: Objective → Results figure → Mechanism.
- Companion written report: weekly_progress.qmd.
-->

## Agenda — {{WEEK_RANGE}} {.smaller}

1. **{{FINDING_1_HEADLINE}}**
2. **{{FINDING_2_HEADLINE}}**
3. **{{FINDING_3_HEADLINE}}**
4. Anticipated Q&A + next steps

::: {.notes}
- One sentence per finding, in the order they appear.
- Frame the week as a single arc: what we set out to test → what shifted.
- Flag any finding that *narrows* a prior claim; reviewers will ask first.
:::

---

## 1. {{FINDING_1_TITLE}} — Objective & Design

- **Goal**: {{ONE_LINE_QUESTION_THIS_ANSWERS}}
- **Setup**: {{DATASET}} · {{MODEL}} · {{BUDGET}} · {{METRIC}}
- **Compared methods**: {{METHOD_LIST}}
- **Why this metric**: {{ONE_LINE_JUSTIFICATION}}

::: {.notes}
**Goal**
- Restate the question in plain language.
- Name the prior result this builds on or challenges.

**Setup**
- Dataset scale and noise level matter — say them out loud.
- Note any control held fixed across all conditions.

**Method choices**
- One short line per method family being compared.
- Flag baselines vs. our method explicitly.
:::

---

## 1. {{FINDING_1_TITLE}} — Results

![]({{FIGURE_PATH_1}}){width=85%}

- **Axes**: x = {{X_AXIS}}, y = {{Y_AXIS}}
- **Colours**: {{COLOR_LEGEND_BLUE}} | {{COLOR_LEGEND_ORANGE}} | {{COLOR_LEGEND_GREY}}
- **Headline number**: {{OUR_METHOD}} = {{VALUE}} ({{DELTA_VS_BASELINE}})

::: {.notes}
**Reading the figure**
- Walk through axes first, then colour groups.
- Point out the reference line / dashed marker.

**Group-by-group**
- Compare each cluster of bars in the order shown.
- Call out the largest gap and the smallest gap.

**Implication**
- One sentence: what this rules in or out for the headline claim.
:::

---

## 1. {{FINDING_1_TITLE}} — Mechanism

- **Observation**: {{WHAT_WE_SAW}}
- **Why it happens**: {{MECHANISM_IN_ONE_LINE}}
- **What it rules out**: {{COMPETING_HYPOTHESIS_KILLED}}

::: {.notes}
**Observation → Explanation**
- Cite the row / number from the previous slide.
- Bridge to the mechanism without inventing new variables.

**Falsifier check**
- State the alternative hypothesis this evidence kills.
- Note any residual ambiguity for the Q&A slide.

**Implication**
- One sentence linking the mechanism back to the headline claim.
:::

---

## 2. {{FINDING_2_TITLE}} — Objective & Design

- **Goal**: {{ONE_LINE_QUESTION}}
- **Design**: {{N}} conditions × {{M}} datasets · {{MODEL}}
  - **{{COND_A}}** — {{ONE_LINE_DESCRIPTION}}
  - **{{COND_B}}** — {{ONE_LINE_DESCRIPTION}}
  - **{{COND_CONTROL}}** — {{ONE_LINE_DESCRIPTION}}

::: {.notes}
**Goal**
- This finding usually isolates a confound; name the confound directly.

**Design**
- Explain what is held fixed and what varies — in that order.
- Map the on-slide condition labels to their figure labels.
:::

---

## 2. {{FINDING_2_TITLE}} — Results

![]({{FIGURE_PATH_2}}){width=85%}

- **Left panel**: {{LEFT_PANEL_DESCRIPTION}}
- **Right panel**: {{RIGHT_PANEL_DESCRIPTION}}
- **Key shift**: {{HEADLINE_SHIFT_IN_NUMBERS}}

::: {.notes}
**Left panel**
- Walk axes; call out the bar that moved most.
- State the absolute value, not just the direction.

**Right panel**
- Connect to the left: same axis or different?
- Read the threshold or reference line out loud.

**Implication**
- Frame as: confound contributes X%, residual is Y, therefore signal is Z.
:::

---

## 3. {{FINDING_3_TITLE}} — Dynamics

- **Goal**: extend a static snapshot to a time-resolved view
- **Conditions**:
  - **{{COND_FULL}}** — full system
  - **{{COND_FROZEN}}** — adaptive component disabled
  - **{{COND_NONE}}** — control, no intervention
- **Probe**: {{METRIC}} measured every {{INTERVAL}}

::: {.notes}
**Why time-resolved**
- Static snapshots hide collapse and recovery dynamics.
- Time view lets us separate "preserves" from "improves".

**Conditions**
- The frozen-vs-full pair isolates the value of adaptation.
- The no-intervention control shows what training does on its own.
:::

---

## 3. {{FINDING_3_TITLE}} — Trajectories

![]({{FIGURE_PATH_3}}){width=85%}

- **Curves**: {{COND_FULL}} ↑ from {{X}} to {{Y}} | {{COND_FROZEN}} ↑ from {{X}} to {{Y}} | {{COND_NONE}} ↓ from {{X}} to {{Y}}
- **Pairwise reads**:
  - Full vs. None → isolates the **presence** of intervention
  - Full vs. Frozen → isolates **adaptation** on top of intervention

::: {.notes}
**Reading the trajectories**
- Read each curve's start, mid, end value.
- Name the monotonic vs. collapsing curve explicitly.

**Pairwise contrasts**
- Full vs. None → the intervention is necessary.
- Full vs. Frozen → adaptation is or is not load-bearing.

**Implication**
- Narrow the claim to what the contrasts actually support.
:::

---

## Anticipated Q&A — *"Why does {{KNOWN_WEAKNESS}} not break this?"* {.smaller}

**Short answer**: most of the gain comes from {{REAL_DRIVER}}, not from {{SUSPECTED_DRIVER}}.

- **Known issue**: {{ISSUE_DESCRIPTION}} — still holds
- **Isolated contrast**: {{COMPONENT_ALONE}} = {{SMALL_DELTA}}, joined = {{BIG_DELTA}}
- **Mechanism**: {{REAL_DRIVER}} carries the load (see [§3](#))
- **Side benefit**: {{REAL_DRIVER}} also partially cancels {{KNOWN_WEAKNESS}}
- **Headroom**: fixing {{KNOWN_WEAKNESS}} is upside, not a blocker

::: {.notes}
**Why pre-empt this**
- This is the sharpest reviewer-style question this week — answer before being asked.

**Numbers to drop**
- Component-alone delta vs. joined delta — show the gap is large.
- Quote the headline mechanism number from the trajectories slide.

**Implication**
- Current headline result was reached *despite* the weakness, not because of it.
:::

---

## Next Steps {.smaller}

**Glossary** (terms used above):

- **{{TERM_1}}**: {{ONE_LINE_DEFINITION}}
- **{{TERM_2}}**: {{ONE_LINE_DEFINITION}}

**Priorities**:

| | Task | Content |
|---|---|---|
| ① | {{TASK_1}} | {{ONE_LINE_PURPOSE}} |
| ② | {{TASK_2}} | {{ONE_LINE_PURPOSE}} |
| ③ | {{TASK_3}} | *Headroom* — {{WHY_NOT_BLOCKER}} |
| ④ | {{TASK_4}} | *Headroom* — {{WHY_NOT_BLOCKER}} |

::: {.notes}
**Glossary**
- Define any term introduced this week before listing priorities.
- Skip terms the audience has heard for ≥ 2 weeks.

**Priorities**
- ① and ② are blockers; ③ and ④ are headroom.
- Note dependencies between items (e.g. ③ unblocks ①).
:::

---

## Closing

**Outer loop**: {{ONE_LINE_OUTER_LOOP_PROGRESS}}

**Inner loop**: {{ONE_LINE_MECHANISTIC_PROGRESS}} — and both findings forced us to **narrow** the claim, not just confirm it.

> **{{ONE_SENTENCE_TAKEAWAY}}**

::: {.notes}
**Outer loop**
- The "did we still win the benchmark" half of the week.

**Inner loop**
- The mechanistic / why-it-works half of the week.
- Emphasise that narrowing a claim *is* progress.

**Implication**
- One sentence the audience should remember a week from now.
:::
